understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate selectable pieces (P and S in the semantic grid) to reach goal tiles marked with '+' symbols. The game required pushing or positioning pushable blocks (B) onto goal positions to complete each level. Progress through multiple levels (tracked by level.current/level.total) would result in a WIN condition once all levels were solved.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement works on a 3-pixel grid system where each MOVE action shifts the selected piece exactly one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. The game contains multiple selectable pieces that can be switched between using CLICK with target coordinates. Pushable blocks adjacent in the movement direction would move along with the selected piece. Walls (#) blocked movement based on their configuration. The movement.{direction}_blocked hints indicated nearby obstacles but weren't absolute predictors. The selected piece (P) could be controlled with directional moves, and clicking on other selectables (S) or the current piece switched control. Each level had a finite step budget tracked by resources.steps_remaining. The recent actions show extensive back-and-forth movement that consumed all 10 remaining steps without achieving a WIN, suggesting the puzzle solution may have been attempted unsuccessfully or the optimal path was not found with the remaining budget.
